mnemonics.title: ACID Properties
mnemonics.content: A-C-I-D: All Cats In Danger (Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability)

mnemonics.title: Transaction States
mnemonics.content: A-P-F-C: Active → Partially Committed → Failed → Committed (flow through states)

mnemonics.title: 2PL Phases
mnemonics.content: G-S: Growing then Shrinking - Can't Grow after you Shrink

mnemonics.title: SQL Update Commands
mnemonics.content: I-U-D: Insert Creates, Update Modifies, Delete Removes - I nsert U pdate D elete

acronyms.usage: Fundamental properties that guarantee reliable transaction processing in DBMS
Concurrency control protocol ensuring serializable schedules
Standard language for managing relational databases
Commands like CREATE, ALTER, DROP that define database structure
Commands like INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE that manipulate data
Commands like COMMIT, ROLLBACK, SAVEPOINT for transaction management
Technique allowing concurrent reads without locks

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circle with arrows: Active → Partially Committed → Committed, and from Failed → Aborted. Show how COMMIT flows to Terminated, while ROLLBACK from Failed goes to Aborted.
Create a 2x2 matrix: S-S = Compatible, S-X = Conflict, X-S = Conflict, X-X = Conflict. Remember: Only Shared with Shared is allowed.
Draw a timeline: Growing phase (upward slope, locks increase) → Point of maximum locks → Shrinking phase (downward slope, locks decrease). No new locks after shrinking starts.
Think of a water pipeline: Atomicity (all water or none), Consistency (clean water in, clean water out), Isolation (separate pipes), Durability (water stored in tank).

analogies.analogy: Think of a bank ATM transaction: withdrawing money involves checking balance, dispensing cash, and updating account - all must happen together or not at all.
Two people each holding one resource and waiting for the other's resource - like two cars at an intersection each waiting for the other to move.
Like a video game save point - you can continue from that checkpoint rather than starting the entire game over if things go wrong.
Like a bouncer at a club checking ID - if you don't meet the criteria (constraints), you can't enter (operation fails).
